title: Measuring competitiveness in a granular and global world (chapter)
outlet: Bruegel Blueprint #24 — Measuring competitiveness in Europe
summary_en: Opening chapter of Blueprint XXIV. Argues for a granular and global view of competitiveness: aggregate measures average over the relevant heterogeneity, and trade matters because it concentrates competitiveness pressure on specific firms and products. Lays the groundwork for the firm-level chapters that follow.
policy_relevance: Methodological frame for the European Commission's competitiveness scorecards and the MNB's productivity work: headline TFP and ULC numbers should be supplemented with within-country dispersion measures and trade-exposed-segment indicators. Caveat: a methodological piece, not a policy-recommendations chapter.
